title: AI Swarm Simulator
creator: Agent Buildprint
summary: AI Swarm Simulator Buildprint remapped from MiroFish as a sleek, clickable swarm-simulation workbench with scenario intake, open-source graph memory, dynamic LLM providers, simulation runtime, report generation, and interaction console.
plainDescription: A source-independent executable Buildprint remapped from MiroFish. It preserves the motion-rich, Canva-like product feel while replacing the original Zep Cloud dependency with an open-source graph-memory adapter path and dynamic OpenAI-compatible LLM provider configuration.
promise: Build a credible MiroFish-inspired AI swarm/social simulation workbench: seed intake, ontology, clickable graph/canvas memory, simulation preparation and runtime traces, report generation, interaction, persistence, dynamic provider setup, and honest provider/runtime blockers.
whatYouGet: Executable Buildprint packet with BUILDPRINT.md canonical start
A generated agent prompt that frames the task as senior product/design alignment, not checklist compliance
A v4 review/handover packet shape with 04-review.md and 05-handover.md, not obsolete evidence-ledger proof theater
Explicit requirements to preserve MiroFish’s sleek, clickable, motion-rich UI/UX instead of flattening it into a static dashboard
Open-source graph-memory replacement path for the original Zep dependency
Dynamic OpenAI-compatible LLM provider configuration requirements
whatYouNeed: LLM provider credentials for live ontology/profile/report proof, or explicit blocked-provider state
A local/open graph-memory runtime such as a Graphiti-compatible adapter with open graph database backing, or an honest local-runtime blocker
OASIS/CAMEL-compatible runtime or approved local deterministic simulation worker
Browser/e2e environment for graph interaction, persistence, report, and console review
includes: 02-project-setup.md source-evidenced setup and Dom-specific remap constraints
Graph and provider boundary phase with open-source graph-memory replacement
Final critical review and handover phase
risks: Flattening the MiroFish experience into a static admin dashboard instead of preserving the sleek motion-rich clickable UX
Requiring Zep Cloud instead of providing an open-source graph-memory adapter path
Hardcoding a single LLM vendor instead of dynamic OpenAI-compatible provider settings
Collapsing the graph workbench into token bubbles, chips, cards, or static SVG decoration
Canned ontology/report/simulation outputs that do not change with inputs
Dead buttons or fake controls presented as completed product behavior
Counting deterministic sandbox adapters as live provider proof
Omitting provider adapters because credentials are missing
Raw debug/admin surfaces standing in for product UX
Secret leakage through logs, reports, screenshots, or handover notes
checks: generated/agent-prompt.md is alignment speech and includes anti-slop review
Packet names https://github.com/666ghj/MiroFish as the inspected origin
Open-source graph-memory replacement is required; Zep Cloud is not required
Dynamic OpenAI-compatible LLM provider settings are required
Graph/canvas UX explicitly requires clickable, inspectable, motion-rich behavior
Final critical reviewer checks dead buttons, placeholders, canned outputs, persistence, and graph-workbench quality

trustBadges.label: Executable packet
trustBadges.detail: The remapped packet passes the Mapper executable-integrity check.

trustBadges.label: MiroFish origin
trustBadges.detail: The package is remapped from https://github.com/666ghj/MiroFish.

trustBadges.label: Open graph memory
trustBadges.detail: The implementation must replace Zep Cloud with an open-source graph-memory adapter path.

trustBadges.label: Dynamic LLM providers
trustBadges.detail: The packet requires dynamic OpenAI-compatible LLM provider configuration.

trustBadges.label: Anti-slop review
trustBadges.detail: Final handover requires a harsh product-quality review after build/test/lint proof.

copyPrompt: Use the AI Swarm Simulator Buildprint remapped from https://github.com/666ghj/MiroFish. First bootstrap exact snapshots: agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/ai-swarm-simulator/package.json . If agb is not installed, clone https://github.com/DomEscobar/agent-buildprint and run node agent-buildprint/bin/agb.js start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/ai-swarm-simulator/package.json . Then read .buildprint/next-agent.md and continue. Do not write Buildprint snapshots manually. Preserve the sleek Canva-like motion/clickable MiroFish UX, replace Zep Cloud with an open-source graph-memory adapter, and make LLM provider configuration dynamic/OpenAI-compatible.

publicStatus.label: v4 remapped packet
publicStatus.explanation: Fresh v4 review/handover packet remapped from MiroFish with open-source graph-memory replacement and dynamic LLM provider requirements; live providers remain honest blockers when unavailable.
